articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Native Multimodal Reasoning and Extended Thinking
articles.summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5, featuring deeply integrated multimodal reasoning across text, image, audio, and video inputs with a unified architecture rather than bolted-on adapters. The model demonstrates significant improvements in multi-step scientific reasoning and long-horizon planning tasks, narrowing the gap with domain-specific expert systems. Enterprise API access rolled out with new structured output guarantees and a 1M-token context window.

articles.title: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics Model Enables Zero-Shot Manipulation in Unstructured Environments
articles.summary: DeepMind published results from its Gemini Robotics foundation model, demonstrating that a single vision-language-action model can perform complex manipulation tasks in cluttered, previously unseen environments without task-specific fine-tuning. The system achieves 87% success on a standardized benchmark of 150 household tasks, a major leap from the sub-50% rates seen just 18 months ago. The work points toward general-purpose robotic assistants that can be deployed without per-site engineering.

articles.title: Figure and BMW Expand Humanoid Robot Deployment to Full Assembly Line Integration
articles.summary: Figure AI announced that its humanoid robots are now operating across multiple stations on BMW's Spartanburg assembly line, moving beyond the initial pilot phase that began in 2024. The robots handle sub-assembly tasks including wire harness routing and panel insertion, working alongside human operators with real-time safety co-monitoring. BMW reports a 17% throughput improvement on the integrated stations.

articles.title: Anthropic Introduces Constitutional Classifiers to Defend Against Jailbreaks at Scale
articles.summary: Anthropic released a detailed technical report on its constitutional classifiers system, a scalable defense layer that reduced successful jailbreak rates on Claude to under 2% across adversarial red-team evaluations. The approach trains lightweight classifier heads using constitutionally-generated synthetic attack data, avoiding the brittleness of rule-based filters. The system is now available as a configurable safety API for enterprise customers building on Claude.

articles.title: TSMC Begins Volume Production of 1.4nm-Class Process Node for Next-Gen AI Accelerators
articles.summary: TSMC confirmed that its A14 process node has entered high-volume manufacturing at its Kaohsiung fab, with first silicon destined for next-generation AI training accelerators from major hyperscaler clients. The node uses gate-all-around nanosheet transistors and backside power delivery, achieving a 20% speed improvement and 30% power reduction over N2. This sets the stage for a new class of AI chips expected to ship in late 2026 data center hardware.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins: First Compliance Audits Target High-Risk Deployments
articles.summary: The European AI Office commenced its first formal compliance audits under the AI Act, focusing on high-risk AI systems in hiring, credit scoring, and law enforcement across multiple member states. Companies face requirements for technical documentation, bias testing, and human oversight mechanisms, with non-compliance penalties reaching 7% of global revenue. The audits are being closely watched as a bellwether for how prescriptive AI regulation will function in practice.

articles.title: Meta Releases Open-Source Llama 4 Behemoth with 2 Trillion Parameters Under Permissive License
articles.summary: Meta released Llama 4 Behemoth, a 2-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model, under a broadly permissive license that allows commercial use without royalty requirements. Benchmarks show it performing competitively with GPT-5 and Gemini Ultra on reasoning and code generation, significantly raising the ceiling for open-weight models. The release includes optimized inference code for multi-node setups and quantized variants that run on 8xH100 clusters.

articles.title: Boston Dynamics and Hyundai Unveil Electric Atlas for Commercial Warehouse Operations
articles.summary: Boston Dynamics officially launched the commercial version of its electric Atlas humanoid robot, purpose-built for warehouse logistics in partnership with Hyundai's supply chain division. The robot features hot-swappable battery packs for continuous operation, integrated LiDAR-vision SLAM for dynamic environment mapping, and a manipulation payload of 25 kg per arm. Initial deployments are scheduled at Hyundai and Kia parts distribution centers in South Korea and the U.S.
